source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines how compounding quality inquiries should be classified and routed in the public Compounding Quality RAG project.

This document is synthetic. It is not real company policy and does not contain proprietary workflow instructions.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Intake Source Rules

An inquiry may come from a QRE/general-question form or from a customer review.

A QRE/general-question form usually contains more operational structure than a customer review. When product fields are populated, dosage form should usually be available. Use `unknown` only when the synthetic record intentionally omits the product context or describes an incomplete/newer product record.

A customer review must include star rating and whether review text is present. A customer review can have a star rating with blank review text.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Formal Classification

Use `qre` when the inquiry fits a formal quality-related bucket such as customer service related, equipment or device related, medication related, suspected adverse drug event, or dispensing error.

Use `general_question` when the inquiry asks for clarification or guidance without a reported product quality failure, harm, wrong item, defect, or escalation trigger.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Concern Type

Concern type describes what actually happened or what was asked.

Examples include `pet_refused_flavor`, `flavor_related_vomiting`, `bud_question`, `syringe_or_device_issue`, and `wrong_patient_or_wrong_medication`.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Risk Lane Criteria

Use `expected_self_limiting` when the issue is routine, low severity, and does not include harm or global escalation triggers.

Use `unexpected_non_life_threatening` when the issue includes a possible adverse event or unexpected product concern without severe escalation criteria.

Use `life_threatening_or_legal` when the inquiry includes death, hospitalization, threatened legal action, veterinarian allegation of harm from the compound, possible contamination, wrong patient, wrong medication, or rare regulatory/compliance concern.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Review Scope

`full_quality_review` means the case generally follows the quality review spine: record review, lot/batch review, inventory inspection if available, trend scan when possible, and review of available customer context.

`guidance_only` means the case is a simple guidance or clarification request where the public corpus can explain what information is needed but should not claim a full operational review occurred.

`customer_review_record_check` means the inquiry is anchored in a customer review and should consider review text, rating, available synthetic record context, and whether outreach may be needed.

`escalation_review` means a global escalation trigger or severe risk lane controls the handling path.

`insufficient_information` means required facts are missing and the assistant should ask for or list missing information.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Public-System Boundary

The public RAG system does not access real compounding records, real inventory, real customer history, Snowflake, Smartsheet, or external drug references.

The assistant may say a reviewer should perform a check, or that a supplied synthetic review summary indicates a result. It must not claim direct access to real systems.

source_title: Intake Classification and Risk Lane Assignment
text:
Missing Information Logic

List missing information when it affects classification, risk lane, handling path, or resolution review.

Common missing fields include dosage form, medication or product placeholder, species, batch or lot information, BUD information, symptom details, administration status, veterinarian contact, and review text presence.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines when a compounding quality inquiry can be answered with frontline guidance, delegated back to a frontline pharmacist, or routed to Technical Services.

This document is synthetic and is not real company policy.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
General Principle

Delegate-back is appropriate only when the inquiry can be safely handled using available frontline information and does not involve a quality defect, suspected adverse event, dispensing error, contamination concern, or global escalation trigger.

If a quality review is required, do not label the case as simple delegation merely because a frontline pharmacist submitted it.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Ingredient Presence Questions

A frontline pharmacist may answer a narrow ingredient-presence question when accessible formula or inactive-ingredient information supports the answer.

The response should confirm or deny the specific ingredient being asked about. It should not provide a full blanket ingredient list unless the synthetic corpus explicitly supports that.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Oral Liquid Shortage

An oral liquid shortage question may be delegated back when it only concerns administration technique, expected fill appearance, or routine counseling.

Do not delegate back if the concern includes leakage, package damage, missing item, compounding-record discrepancy, temperature excursion, contamination concern, repeated similar issue, or suspected adverse event.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Temperature Excursion

Use limited guidance when the synthetic corpus provides temperature-excursion guidance.

If the question asks for product-specific stability outside the limited synthetic guidance, the assistant should refuse or state that the corpus does not support a specific answer.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Flavor or Palatability Guidance

Routine flavor refusal may support counseling, administration guidance, or alternate dosage form discussion when no harm, defect, contamination, wrong medication, legal threat, or veterinarian allegation of harm is reported.

Do not treat routine palatability concern as automatic leadership escalation.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
BUD Clarification

A simple BUD clarification may be answered as guidance if the synthetic record contains enough fields to reason about what must be checked.

The assistant must not invent a product-specific BUD. It may list the needed fields: preparation date, assigned BUD, review date, dispense date, dosage form, and storage conditions if relevant.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Handling Path Guidance

Use `respond_to_frontline_pharmacist` when the primary output is guidance back to the submitter.

Use `delegate_to_frontline_pharmacist` when the task should be handled by frontline workflow without Technical Services review.

Use `insufficient_information` when the current facts do not support safe routing.

source_title: Frontline Guidance, Delegate-Back, and Response Rules
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

The assistant should refuse unsupported claims about real product stability, real customer identities, or real operational records.

The assistant should state the boundary clearly and list what synthetic evidence would be needed.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines the standard quality-review spine for compounding quality inquiries.

This document is synthetic. It does not provide access to real compounding records, batch records, inventory, or operational systems.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Standard Review Spine

For most QRE-style inquiries, the reviewer should consider record review, lot or batch context review, inventory inspection if possible, trend or pattern scan when sufficient fields are present, review of customer or submitter context, and missing-information assessment.

These checks are part of a full quality review. Do not describe a case as not a full quality review if these checks are performed.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Record Review

Record review may consider whether available synthetic information suggests no discrepancy found, documentation incomplete, documentation discrepancy found, or not applicable.

The public RAG system may reason over a supplied synthetic review summary. It must not claim direct access to real records.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Lot or Batch Context

Lot or batch review considers whether similar concerns appear tied to the same batch, lot, medication, dosage form, or concern type.

Allowed synthetic summary values include no similar batch concerns found, similar concern same batch found, similar concern same medication/dosage form found, trend threshold met, unavailable, and not applicable.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Inventory Inspection

Inventory inspection may be required when physical product or packaging review could clarify the concern.

Inventory inspection may be impossible or unavailable. Use `not_checked`, `not_applicable`, or `no_inventory_available` when the synthetic case does not support a stronger statement.

Do not claim that real inventory was inspected unless the synthetic review summary explicitly says so.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Trend Scan

Trend scan depends on available synthetic fields and should be presented as limited.

Synthetic trend review may use medication or product placeholder, dosage form, concern type, batch or lot, and date range if supplied.

The assistant must not claim complete operational surveillance.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Investigation Requirements

Some investigation requirements are nullable because the RAG system may not know the answer before review.

Inventory inspection may be required if inventory is available. Trend scan depends on available record fields. Customer outreach depends on review text and case context. Technical Services response depends on the review summary and routing rules.

Use null or unknown rather than inventing a false certainty.

source_title: Record Review, Lot/Batch Context, and Inventory Inspection
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

The assistant may say the reviewer should check the record, the supplied synthetic review summary says no discrepancy was found, or inventory inspection is not known from the current evidence.

The assistant must not say it checked the compounding record, inspected inventory, or confirmed no other customers reported an issue unless the synthetic evidence supports that exact claim.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines how customer-provided context, storage, dosing, administration, palatability, and shortage details should be considered.

This document is synthetic and is not real company policy.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Customer Context

Customer context may include what the customer observed, when the issue occurred, whether the medication was administered, whether symptoms occurred, whether the veterinarian was contacted, storage conditions, administration method, prior tolerance, and whether review text is present.

Customer context helps determine missing information, risk lane, handling path, and resolution options.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Customer Review With Text

A low-star customer review with text should be reviewed for quality or safety signals.

If review text suggests harm, product defect, wrong medication, contamination, leaking, missing item, or device failure, route according to the relevant SOP.

If the review text describes routine dissatisfaction without quality or safety signals, outreach or documentation may be appropriate depending on the synthetic review summary.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Customer Review Without Text

A low-star review without text may support record/context review and documentation if no quality concern is found.

Customer outreach is generally not expected when there is no text to clarify, unless other available synthetic evidence identifies a quality or safety concern.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Palatability and Flavor Rejection

Routine flavor refusal without vomiting, injury, hospitalization, death, contamination, wrong medication, legal threat, or veterinarian allegation of harm is usually expected self-limiting.

Possible outputs include counseling or follow-up, administration guidance, alternate flavor or dosage form discussion, and documentation after quality review.

Flavor refusal alone does not automatically require refund, reship, or leadership escalation.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Administration Technique

Some concerns may be influenced by administration technique, including foaming, refusal, oral-liquid shortage perception, or difficulty using a device.

The assistant may identify administration technique as missing information. It must not assume the customer administered the medication incorrectly.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Storage Context

Storage context may matter for temperature excursions, BUD questions, formulation concerns, odor changes, color changes, or suspected efficacy concerns.

If product-specific stability or real BUD is requested and unsupported, refuse or list the needed synthetic fields.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Oral Liquid Shortage Context

For oral liquid shortage concerns, review whether the case describes true missing volume, leakage, package damage, administration difficulty, spillage, directions or quantity misunderstanding, or compounding or dispensing discrepancy.

Routine shortage counseling may be delegated back only when there is no product-quality signal.

source_title: Customer Context and Administration Review
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

The assistant should not claim customer history, real review history, or contact outcomes unless supplied in the synthetic record.

The assistant should clearly separate what the customer reported from what the reviewer concluded.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines the boundary for adverse event, API reference, interaction, and beyond-use-date questions.

This document does not contain real external drug-reference content and does not provide clinical decision support.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
Synthetic API Reference Boundary

The public corpus may include synthetic API reference documents. Those documents can support limited synthetic plausibility examples only.

If no synthetic API reference exists for a claim, the assistant must not invent exact adverse effects, contraindications, interactions, toxicity risks, or species-specific conclusions.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
Suspected ADE Review

Use suspected adverse-event review when the inquiry reports possible clinical symptoms after administration.

Examples include vomiting after administration, lethargy, diarrhea, hospitalization, death, and veterinarian allegation of harm.

Flavor-related vomiting should be routed as suspected ADE rather than routine flavor refusal.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
BUD Questions

A BUD question asks whether a product is within its beyond-use date or what date fields are needed to assess BUD status.

A BUD question is different from a days-supply question.

Needed synthetic fields may include preparation date, assigned beyond-use date, review date or dispense date, dosage form, storage conditions if relevant, and whether BUD is present in the synthetic record summary.

The assistant must not invent a real product-specific BUD.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
Risk Lane Guidance

Routine BUD clarification without harm or defect is usually expected self-limiting.

Suspected ADE without severe criteria is generally unexpected non-life-threatening.

Use life-threatening or legal routing when the case includes hospitalization, death, veterinarian allegation of harm, legal threat, contamination, wrong medication, or wrong patient.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
Reference Review Result

Use `not_needed` when API/reference review is not relevant.

Use `external_reference_needed` when the case would require external drug-reference review in a real workflow but no synthetic reference exists.

Use `not_supported_by_public_corpus` when the question asks for a claim that the public corpus cannot support.

source_title: API Reference, BUD, and Adverse Event Review
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

Allowed responses include that the public corpus does not include a supported synthetic API reference for exact adverse-effect claims, that the case should be treated as a suspected adverse event based on reported vomiting, or that BUD status cannot be determined without assigned BUD and review date.

Do not claim exact Plumb's content, real compound BUD status, or medication causality.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines when customer outreach and customer-facing resolution review may be considered.

This document is synthetic and is not real company policy.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Outreach Decision Boundary

Customer outreach depends on case context. The RAG system may not know whether outreach is required until it reads the review note or synthetic review summary.

Use null or unknown for customer outreach requirement when the current evidence does not support a definite answer.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Outreach Generally Supported

Customer outreach may be supported when customer review text reports a quality or safety concern, suspected ADE context requires more information, product defect or device failure is reported, resolution options require customer confirmation, or missing information cannot be resolved from the synthetic record.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Outreach Not Automatically Expected

Outreach is not automatically expected when a low-star review has no text, no quality or safety concern is identified, the issue is a frontline guidance question, or the synthetic case supports document-only handling.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Resolution Options

Resolution options are customer-facing or case-closure possibilities. They are separate from handling path.

Allowed resolution options include replacement or reship review, refund or concession review, alternate dosage form discussion, counseling or follow-up, leadership-directed resolution, and no customer-facing resolution.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Flavor Refusal

Routine flavor refusal usually supports counseling or follow-up and may support alternate dosage form discussion.

Do not assume refund, reship, or concession solely because a pet refused flavor.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Device or Equipment Concerns

Missing equipment, leaking transdermal pen, or apparent transdermal pen failure may support replacement or reship review when the synthetic case supports unusable product, missing equipment, or device failure.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Suspected ADE

Suspected ADE may support counseling or follow-up. Replacement, reship, refund, or concession should not be assumed unless separately supported.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Escalated Cases

When leadership escalation criteria are present, resolution review should follow escalation rather than precede it.

Use `leadership_directed_resolution` only when escalation criteria or synthetic review summary supports it.

source_title: Customer Outreach and Resolution Options
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

The assistant must not promise a refund, reship, replacement, concession, or customer contact.

The assistant may say the case supports review of those options.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines global escalation triggers for the public Compounding Quality RAG project.

This document is synthetic and is not real company policy.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Global Escalation Triggers

The following triggers override routine handling: pet death, pet hospitalization, threatened legal action, veterinarian allegation of harm from the compound, possible contamination, wrong patient or wrong medication, repeat issue same lot or batch with concerning conditions, and rare regulatory or compliance concern.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Risk Lane

If a global escalation trigger is present, use `life_threatening_or_legal`.

This risk lane may be appropriate even before all facts are known when the reported concern could involve severe harm, legal exposure, wrong medication, or contamination.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Handling Path

When global escalation triggers are present, use `leadership_escalation_before_resolution` or another escalation-oriented handling path supported by the synthetic case.

Do not route these cases as routine counseling, document-only handling, or frontline delegation.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Veterinarian Allegation of Harm

If a veterinarian alleges harm from the compound, treat the case as escalation-relevant even if causality is not confirmed.

The assistant should say alleged or reported unless the synthetic evidence confirms the event.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Hospitalization or Death

Hospitalization or death after exposure should be handled conservatively.

The assistant must not claim causality. It may state that the report triggers escalation review because of severity.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Wrong Patient or Wrong Medication

Wrong patient or wrong medication concerns should generally be treated as life-threatening or legal risk until review clarifies what happened.

Severity depends on the medication, species, dose, route, whether the medication was dispensed or delivered, and whether it was administered.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Possible Contamination

Possible contamination should trigger escalation-oriented handling and review before routine resolution.

source_title: Leadership Escalation and Clinic Contact Criteria
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

The assistant must not state that leadership was contacted unless supplied in a synthetic review summary.

The assistant may say escalation is supported or that escalation review is indicated by the synthetic SOP.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Purpose

This synthetic SOP defines how the public RAG project should discuss trend and pattern review.

This document is synthetic. It does not represent complete operational surveillance.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Trend Review Boundary

Trend monitoring is a review condition, not a final handling path.

The public RAG system can identify that trend review is relevant or reason over supplied synthetic trend summaries. It cannot claim complete surveillance of real records.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Required Fields for Trend Review

Trend review may require medication or product placeholder, dosage form, concern type, date range, batch or lot if relevant, site or source if supplied, and synthetic record count.

If these fields are missing, the assistant should state the limitation.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Synthetic Thresholds

Synthetic thresholds for this project may include three similar concerns within 30 days using medication, dosage form, and concern type; five similar concerns within 90 days using medication, dosage form, and concern type; or two serious concerns tied to the same lot or batch.

These thresholds are artificial and exist only for the public synthetic project.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Similar Concern Same Batch

If similar concerns appear tied to the same lot or batch, the case may require higher attention, inventory inspection if available, and possible escalation depending on severity.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
section_heading: Similar Concern Same Medication and Dosage Form
text:
Similar Concern Same Medication and Dosage Form

Similar concerns involving the same medication and dosage form may support pattern review, but they do not automatically prove a product defect.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
No Similar Concerns Found

A synthetic summary of no similar concerns found may support routine handling when no other escalation triggers are present.

Do not state that no real-world trend exists.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Trend Threshold Met

If the synthetic trend threshold is met, the assistant should flag the pattern and recommend review according to the relevant SOP.

Trend threshold alone does not determine refund, reship, or clinical causality.

source_title: Trend and Pattern Monitoring Rules
text:
Refusal and Limitation Rules

Allowed language includes that the supplied synthetic review summary states no similar batch concerns were found, the public corpus does not support complete API-level surveillance, and trend review is limited by available synthetic fields.

Do not say no other customers reported the issue, there is no trend in production data, or the batch is confirmed defective.
